description_long: This object consists of a vertical section of decaying wood with three yellowish-brown mushrooms growing from a hollowed cavity. The mushrooms have rounded, slightly convex caps with a scaly, spotted texture and are stacked vertically, emerging from the exposed, rough interior of the wood. The wood itself is rectangular, with a bark-covered exterior and a jagged, uneven opening where the mushrooms protrude. The caps of the mushrooms display a gradient of yellow to brown, with darker spots concentrated toward the center. The surrounding wood is weathered, with visible cracks and a fibrous, organic texture. The overall appearance suggests a natural growth of mushrooms on a decaying tree trunk, commonly found in shaded, moist urban parks or wooded areas.
description: A cluster of three yellow-brown, scaly-capped mushrooms growing from a hollowed, weathered section of tree trunk. The mushrooms emerge from a jagged cavity in the wood, with their caps displaying a spotted texture.
description_view_0: The side of the wood block is visible, with a jagged opening revealing the rough, fibrous interior and a partial view of the mushrooms inside.
description_view_1: The front view shows the three mushrooms clearly, stacked vertically and protruding from the hollowed cavity in the wood.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the wood block is shown, with the edges of the mushroom caps just visible, emphasizing the thickness of the wood.
description_view_3: The back of the wood block is mostly solid and unbroken, with no visible mushrooms or openings.
